title: You sound like a local
setting: At the market, a vendor who's never met you is surprised by how you speak.

steps.speaker: Vendedora
steps.es: Oiga, ¿y usted de qué parte es? Habla raro pero bonito.
steps.en: Say, where are you from? You talk funny but nice.

options.es: Soy gringo, pero ya medio pinolero, jaja.
options.en: I'm a gringo, but half-Nicaraguan now, haha.
options.feedback: Charming and self-aware — claiming 'pinolero' playfully will delight her.
You clearly do! Don't undersell yourself when someone's complimenting your Spanish.

options.es: De los Estados Unidos, pero vivo aquí.
options.en: From the US, but I live here.
options.feedback: Clear and warm. Mentioning you live here explains the local flavor in your speech.

steps.speaker: Vendedora
steps.es: ¡No me diga! Si habla igualito que nosotros.
steps.en: You don't say! You talk just like us.

options.es: Es que aprendí en la calle, con la gente.
options.en: It's because I learned in the street, with the people.
options.feedback: A lovely, true answer — it honors the people who taught you. She'll appreciate it.

options.en: Thanks, it's taken me a lot of effort.
options.feedback: Humble and honest. Acknowledging the work behind it is genuine and relatable.
Bragging deflates the warm moment. Humility wins hearts here, not boasting.

steps.speaker: Vendedora
steps.es: Qué lindo. ¿Y le gusta vivir en Nicaragua?
steps.en: How nice. And do you like living in Nicaragua?

options.es: Me enamoré del país y de su gente, doña.
options.en: I fell in love with the country and its people, ma'am.
options.feedback: Heartfelt and respectful — 'doña' plus genuine warmth. She'll remember you.

options.es: Sí, este lugar ya es mi hogar.
options.en: Yes, this place is my home now.
options.feedback: Simple and moving. 'Mi hogar' carries real weight after this whole journey.
Lukewarm after she's been so warm! If you love it, let her hear it.

steps.speaker: Vendedora
steps.es: Pues bienvenido, pinolero adoptado. Aquí tiene su casa.
steps.en: Well, welcome, adopted Nicaraguan. Here you have your home.

options.es: Gracias, doña. Eso significa todo para mí.
options.en: Thank you, ma'am. That means everything to me.
options.feedback: The perfect close to the whole journey — gratitude, warmth, belonging. Beautiful.

options.es: Qué amable. Me hace sentir en familia.
options.en: How kind. You make me feel like family.
options.feedback: Warm and genuine — naming the feeling of family lands beautifully.
A little abrupt for such a tender welcome. Let the moment breathe before you go.
